new_issues.title: Task: [frontend] Set or reset a password — account Settings + forgot-password
new_issues.author: sunday19x3
new_issues.body:
sunday19x3
opened
Source
Parent: Vision: Creator Console Dashboard (Vision: Creator Console Dashboard #15)
Related: Task: [frontend] Wire the Continue-with-Google button to BetterAuth social sign-in #72 (Continue-with-Google wiring) — this closes the gap that flow opened.
Goal

An operator can give their account a password and recover a forgotten one, without ever being forced to.

Why

Two holes appeared once Google sign-in shipped:

A Google sign-up has no password at all, so email/password sign-in is permanently closed to them — with no way to open it. If they ever lose access to the Google account, they lose the workspace.
The sign-in form had no recovery path — a forgotten password locked you out with nothing to click.

A rejected alternative: forcing a password at Google sign-up. That defeats the point of social login (the user picked Google precisely to avoid a password), and it weakens security rather than strengthening it — a Google account is already protected by Google's 2FA/risk checks, while a coerced password is the kind that gets reused or written down. The password stays optional.

Mechanic

No new backend endpoint. BetterAuth has no self-serve set-password route, but its /reset-password creates the credential account when one is missing (api/routes/password.mjs) — which is exactly the "add a password" case. So one emailed round-trip serves all three needs: add a first password, change an existing one, recover a forgotten one. Proof of identity is the mailbox — already verified by Google.

Example of done
Settings → Password reads /list-accounts and offers Add a password (Google-only) or Change password, emailing a link to the account address.
The link lands on /reset-password: ?token= shows the form (8-character minimum, show/hide toggle); ?error=INVALID_TOKEN explains the link expired and points back to Settings.
Sign-in → password step offers Forgot password?, emailing the same link (sign-in only — a sign-up has no password to recover).
Signing in with Google never forces a password.
Shipped

On riffables-monorepo main:

2f6061e — /settings + /reset-password, plus the auth-proxy allowlist for list-accounts (GET), request-password-reset and reset-password (POST). Settings is reachable from the workspace switcher; the sidebar keeps its four product surfaces.
4840b4f — forgot-password on the sign-in form.
Note for dev/infra

redirectTo is validated against BETTER_AUTH_TRUSTED_ORIGINS, which currently lists only https://riffables.speedrunlabs.ai. The flow therefore works on the deployed console but returns "Invalid redirectURL" when exercised from http://localhost:3000. If local testing of this flow is wanted, add the localhost origin to that env — no code change needed.

Verified

Preview: both /reset-password states (valid token → form; expired → recovery copy) render and gate correctly; the forgot-password click reaches the backend through the proxy allowlist. eslint + production build pass. The full mail round-trip needs the deployed origin.

new_issues.title: Epic: Operator image asset library — images for site & article content
new_issues.author: phanhoc
new_issues.body:
phanhoc
opened
Parent Vision

Decomposes #16 (Public Audience Site) — the images operators place in their site and article content.

Milestone

V1

Mechanic

An operator uploads an image (a logo, a hero image, an in-article picture) and the platform hands back a permanent public URL they can drop into their site or article content. Uploading the same image again returns the same URL instead of piling up duplicates. Each operator keeps a private library of their own uploads they can browse, label, and remove; removing an image takes it out of the library while any site or article already using it keeps rendering. Images are the operator's own — one operator never sees or touches another's uploads.

operator picks an image ─▶ upload ─▶ gets a permanent public URL
                                        │
       same image again ────────────────┘ (same URL, no duplicate)

library:  browse my images · label · remove (embeds already placed keep working)

What an operator can do
Action	Outcome
Upload an image	Receives a permanent public URL usable anywhere (site content, articles, embeds)
Re-upload the same image	Gets the same URL back — no duplicate is created
Browse their library	Sees only the images they uploaded, newest first
Label an image	Gives an image a human-readable name / alt text
Remove an image	Drops it from the library; already-placed copies keep loading
Acceptance criteria
An operator can upload a PNG, JPEG, WebP, or GIF and immediately receive a public URL that loads the image in any browser.
An upload larger than 10 MB is rejected with a clear error and nothing is stored.
A non-image file — or an SVG — is rejected; only raster images are accepted (the check does not trust the client-declared file type).
Re-uploading a byte-for-byte identical image returns the same existing URL rather than creating a second copy.
An operator listing their images sees only images they uploaded; another operator's images are never listed or reachable through their account.
Each image carries its original filename, type, size, and an editable label.
An operator with many images can page through the full library.
Removing an image takes it out of the operator's library, but a site or article that already embedded that image still renders it (the URL keeps resolving).
Locked constants
Max upload size: 10 MiB
Allowed types: PNG, JPEG, WebP, GIF (raster only; SVG excluded)
Ownership: per-operator (an operator manages only their own uploads), tenant-isolated
URLs: permanent and public — safe to embed in published content
Remove is soft: the stored image stays reachable so already-placed embeds never break
Backend dependencies
Public object storage (the same S3-compatible store — MinIO in dev / R2 in prod — already used to hold ingested audio in Pillar A), configured with a public bucket + public base URL.
Out of scope
Console UI for uploading and picking images inside the site/article editor (a separate frontend Task).
Image processing — resizing, thumbnails, re-encoding, EXIF stripping.
Audience-side uploads (only operators upload; the audience only views).
Non-image assets (documents, video, audio).
Reclaiming storage for removed images / CDN cache purge.
Source
Parent Vision: Vision: Public Audience Site #16 (Public Audience Site)
PRD: PRD.md § 4 High-level design (Creator Console surface; audience site content)
Implementation (separate repo): riffables-monorepo PR Task: Offer a 'View live site' action right after publishing #47 — design + plan at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-15-image-asset-upload-design.md
